zh.Oudenaarde, Oost-Vlaanderen, België: 比利時奧斯吉福拉安德倫奧德納爾德
zh.lifeSummary: 當Arnulf IV van Oudenaarde en Pamele於大約1180年出生在荷蘭霍蘭時，他的父親Gilbert II van Oudenaarde20歲，母親Richilde de Mortagne de Tournai22歲。他於大約1220年與Alix de Rosoy結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1243年享年64歲。

zh-hans.Leuven, Flemish Brabant, Belgium: 比利时福拉阿姆-布拉班特洛文
zh-hans.Oudenaarde, Oost-Vlaanderen, België: 比利时奧斯吉福拉安德倫奧德納爾德
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Arnulf IV van Oudenaarde en Pamele于大约1180年出生在荷兰霍蘭时，他的父亲Gilbert II van Oudenaarde20岁，母亲Richilde de Mortagne de Tournai22岁。他于大约1220年与Alix de Rosoy结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1243年享年64岁。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: en
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Chong, Young, Chun, Hang, Ho, In, Jung, Maeng, Mijin, Rong, Hyong, Pong, Then, Yom, Lanh, Nu.  Cambodian Chea, Chhay, Chheng, Chhom, Voeun, Yoeun.  Spanish Alejandro, Angel, Miguel, Ricardo, Secundino, Tomas.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 阮, see   Ruan   3.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 殷 and 尹, see   Yin   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ruan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Xiu, Ying, Biao, Chao, Cheng, Guang, Guohua, Hua, Huiming, Jia, Jian Hua.  Spanish Jorge, Arnulfo, Arturo, Blanca, Carlos, Eduardo, Gregorio, Guillermo, Javier, Jesus, Jose, Josefina.
Irish and Scottish: variant of   Ruane  .
Spanish (Ruán): nickname from ruán ‘roan’ (a color of horses), a variant of Ruano .

nameMeanings.name: Pamele
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Parmley
meanings: English (mainly Cumberland and Durham): presumably a habitational name, perhaps from a post-medieval form of Parmentley in Whitfield (Northumberland), which is recorded as Parmaly in 1698. The placename appears to derive from Middle English permain ‘pear’ (Old French parmain, permain) + leie ‘clearing, meadow’ (Old English lēah ‘woodland clearing’).

nameMeanings.name: Arnulf
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

nameMeanings.name: IV
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Arnulf IV van Oudenaarde en Pamele was born about 1180, in Holland, Netherlands, his father, Gilbert II van Oudenaarde, was 20 and his mother, Richilde de Mortagne de Tournai, was 22. He married Alix de Rosoy about 1220. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1243, at the age of 64.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Arnulf IV van Oudenaarde en Pamele est né environ 1180, à Holland, Pays-Bas, son père, Gilbert II van Oudenaarde, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Richilde de Mortagne de Tournai, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Alix de Rosoy environ 1220. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1243, à l’âge de 64 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Arnulf IV van Oudenaarde en Pamele um 1180 in Holland, Niederlande geboren wurde, war sein Vater Gilbert II van Oudenaarde 20 und seine Mutter Richilde de Mortagne de Tournai 22. Er hat um 1220 Alix de Rosoy geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1243 im Alter von 64 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Arnulf IV van Oudenaarde en Pamele è nato il circa 1180 nel Holland, Paesi Bassi, il suo padre, Gilbert II van Oudenaarde, aveva 20 anni e il suo madre, Richilde de Mortagne de Tournai, aveva 22 anni. Lui sposato Alix de Rosoy circa 1220. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1243, all'età di 64 anni.

ja.Oudenaarde, Oost-Vlaanderen, België: ベルギーオオスト-vラアンデレンオウデナアルド
ja.lifeSummary: Arnulf IV van Oudenaarde en Pameleが約1180年にオランダホーランドで生まれた時，父Gilbert II van Oudenaardeは20歳，母Richilde de Mortagne de Tournaiは22歳であった。約1220年にAlix de Rosoyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1243年に64歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Arnulf IV van Oudenaarde en Pamele(이)가 대략 1180년 네덜란드 홀랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Gilbert II van Oudenaarde(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Richilde de Mortagne de Tournai(은)는 22세였다. 그는 대략 1220년 Alix de Rosoy(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1243년 64세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Arnulf IV van Oudenaarde en Pamele nasceu aproximadamente 1180, em Holland, Países Baixos, seu pai, Gilbert II van Oudenaarde, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Richilde de Mortagne de Tournai, tinha 22 anos. Ele casou-se com Alix de Rosoy aproximadamente 1220. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1243, com 64 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Arnulf IV van Oudenaarde en Pamele родился около 1180 г. в местности холланд, Нидерланды, его отцу по имени Gilbert II van Oudenaarde было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Richilde de Mortagne de Tournai, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Alix de Rosoy около 1220 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1243 г. в возрасте 64 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Arnulf IV van Oudenaarde en Pamele nació aproximadamente 1180, en Holland, Países Bajos, su papá, Gilbert II van Oudenaarde, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Richilde de Mortagne de Tournai, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Alix de Rosoy aproximadamente 1220. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1243, a la edad de 64 años.
